nodes.label: Migration path triggers
nodes.rationale: Workflow runs on push to main only when supabase/migrations/**, supabase/config.toml, or the workflow file itself change, plus manual workflow_dispatch.

nodes.label: Next.js breaking changes notice
nodes.rationale: This Next.js version has breaking changes vs training data; APIs, conventions, and file structure may differ. Agents must read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing code and heed deprecation notices.

nodes.label: Supabase Auth URL configuration
nodes.rationale: Site URL and Redirect URLs must match the Vercel domain and localhost callback; password reset uses callback with ?next=/reset-password.

nodes.label: SQL migrations apply order
nodes.rationale: Run 001_hub_phase1.sql then 002_profile_names.sql in Supabase SQL Editor; if 001 already applied, run only 002.

nodes.label: DevHub Phase 1 Hub Global
nodes.rationale: Modular mobile-first PWA developer hub with Supabase auth, roles, module shell, settings, and abstracted billing (mock, not Stripe).

nodes.label: MockPaymentProvider
nodes.rationale: getPaymentProvider() returns MockPaymentProvider; Settings Billing mock unlocks Pro and module:ai. Stripe/Lemon Squeezy later via PaymentProvider in src/core/billing/index.ts.

nodes.label: src app core shared structure
nodes.rationale: design-system is mandatory for UI; app routes compose only the design system; core holds auth, billing, entitlements, module-registry; shared/ui holds business forms based on DS.

nodes.label: Token layering primitives semantic theme
nodes.rationale: Primitives have no UI meaning; semantic roles are overridable in .dark; @theme is top-level only with --color-*, --radius-*, --font-* namespaces; pages use utilities or design system, never hard-coded hex.

nodes.label: Design system three layers
nodes.rationale: Tokens CSS in src/assets/styles/; primitives UI via shadcn in src/components/ui/; product API wrappers and business patterns in src/design-system/. Pages must import from @/design-system, not @/components/ui/*.
